title: Extend renderer contract for modular rich/raw document formats
description:
Extend the Web UI document renderer contract so new document formats can register themselves cleanly without adding per-format branching to `DocumentViewer`.

## Background

The existing registry abstraction selects between Markdown and HTML renderers, but the current contract is still too narrow for the next formats. CSV and source-oriented files need matching by path/extension as well as content type, and the viewer shell needs a renderer-driven way to learn whether a renderer supports shared behaviors such as inline raw mode and preview caps.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Renderer selection can match `.csv`, `.txt`, `.rs`, and `.cpp` documents even when the server falls back to generic text-like content types, and this behavior is covered by unit tests.
- [ ] The renderer contract exposes capability metadata needed for modular rich/raw behavior and preview-cap handling without pushing new format-specific branching into `DocumentViewer`.
- [ ] Existing Markdown and HTML renderer selection behavior remains unchanged and is covered by regression tests.
- [ ] `DocumentViewer` remains a renderer-agnostic shell for shared document chrome rather than a switchboard for CSV, TXT, Rust, or C++ behavior.

## Notes

This task establishes the contract and registry foundation that the later renderer tasks build on.
